title: Concealed toilet cistern assembly
abstract: A concealed toilet cistern assembly (1, 101), comprising a cistern (15, 115) which has a water outlet (35) and which is arranged inside a container (9, 109). The container (9, 109) is arranged behind a bathroom wall surface (21, 121) and has a front panel (7, 107) that faces the bathroom wall surface (21, 121). A flush pipe (39, 139) extends from the water outlet (35, 135) inside the container (9, 109), through the front panel (7, 107), such that any leakage occurring either from the cistern (15, 115) or the flush pipe (39, 139) will result in the leaking water being caught in the container (9, 109). The bathroom wall surface (21, 121) has a wall aperture (87, 187). The wall aperture has a wall aperture width (74) which is larger than the cistern width (84). The wall aperture has a wall aperture height (72) which is smaller than the cistern height (82) and larger than the cistern depth (86), such that the cistern (15, 115) can easily be mounted, accessed and replaced without impairing the structure of the bathroom wall.
claims:
Claims Claims 1 . A concealed toilet cistern assembly (1 , 101 ), comprising a cistern (15, 1 15) which has a water outlet (35) and which is arranged inside a container (9, 109), the container (9, 109) is arranged behind a bathroom wall surface (21 , 121 ) and has a front panel (7, 107) that faces the bathroom wall surface (21 , 121 ), wherein a flush pipe (39, 139) extends from the water outlet (35, 135) inside the container (9, 109), through the front panel (7, 107), and wherein the bathroom wall surface (21 , 121 ) has a wall aperture (87, 187), characterized in that the wall aperture (87, 187) - has a wall aperture width (74) which is larger than the cistern width (84); and - has a wall aperture height (72) which is smaller than the cistern height (82) and larger than the cistern depth (86). 2. A concealed toilet cistern assembly (1 , 101 ) according to claim 1 , characterized in that the wall aperture (87, 187) is covered by an operating panel (3, 103) which comprises push buttons (5, 105) adapted for flushing. 3. A concealed toilet cistern assembly (1 , 101 ) according to one of the preceding claims, characterized in that the container (9, 109) comprises a container aperture (23, 123) having an area through which the cistern (15, 1 15) is able to pass, and that the flush pipe (39, 139) extends through said front panel (7, 107) in a position below the container aperture (23, 123). 4. A concealed toilet cistern assembly (1 , 101 ) according to one of the preceding claims, characterized in that the container (9, 109) has a container aperture (23, 123) - which has a container aperture width (24, 124) which is larger than the cistern width (84); and - which has a container aperture height (22, 122) which is smaller than the cistern height (82) and larger than the cistern depth (86). 5. A concealed toilet cistern assembly (1 , 101 ) according to any one of the preceding claims, characterized in that when the cistern (15, 1 15) is in the installed position 

 within the container (9, 109), more than half of its dimension corresponding to its height (82) is below the lower rim (69) of the wall aperture (87, 187). 6. A concealed toilet cistern assembly (1 , 101 ) according to claim 3 and any one of claims 4 to 5, characterized in that the container aperture (23) is an aperture in the front panel (7) of the container (9) and coinciding with the wall aperture (87). 7. A concealed toilet cistern assembly (1 , 101 ) according to one of the preceding claims, characterized in that it comprises a guiding arrangement adapted to guide the cistern (15, 1 15) downwards within the container (9, 109), wherein the guiding arrangement comprises guide protrusions (55) and guide slots (57). 8. A concealed toilet cistern assembly (1 , 101 ) according to one of the preceding claims, characterized in that a flush pipe attachment organ (43) extends between an inner surface of a container panel (7, 27, 29, 31 , 33) and the flush pipe (39, 139), wherein its engagement with the flush pipe (39, 139) is at a distance from the front panel (7, 107). 9. A concealed toilet cistern assembly (1 , 101 ) according to one of the preceding claims, characterized in that the depth (86) of the cistern (15, 1 15) is larger at its bottom portion than at its upper portion. 10. A concealed toilet cistern assembly (1 , 101 ) according to one of the preceding claims, characterized in that the front panel (7, 107) of the container (9, 109) extends in the downwards direction beyond the position of a bottom panel (31 , 131 ) of the container (9, 109). 1 1 . A concealed toilet cistern assembly (1 , 101 ) according to one of the preceding claims, characterized in that the container (9, 109), the flush pipe (39, 139), and at least a portion of the guiding arrangement (57) is provided as one single component. 12. A concealed toilet cistern assembly (1 , 101 ), comprising a cistern (15, 1 15) which has a water outlet (35) and which is arranged inside a container (9, 109), the container (9, 109) is arranged behind a bathroom wall surface (21 , 121 ) and has a 

 front panel (7, 107) that faces the bathroom wall surface (21 , 121 ), wherein a flush pipe (39, 139) extends from the water outlet (35, 135) inside the container (9, 109), through the front panel (7, 107), and wherein the bathroom wall surface (21 , 121 ) has a wall aperture (87, 187), characterized in that the container (9, 109) comprises a container aperture (23, 123) through which the cistern (15, 1 15) is able to pass, and that the flush pipe (39, 139) extends through said front panel (7, 107) in a position below the container aperture (23, 123). 13. A concealed toilet cistern assembly (1 , 101 ) according to claim 12, characterized in that a drain pipe (47) extends from a drain (45) in the container (9, 109) and beyond the bathroom wall surface (21 , 121 ). 14. A concealed toilet cistern assembly (1 , 101 ) according to claim 12, characterized in that a drain pipe (47) extends from a drain (45) in the container (9, 109) and beyond the front panel (7, 107).
